summary: Matched several previously stubbed Yoshi special-move functions in ftYs_SpecialN.c, especially SpecialS/AirSpecialS transition, physics, collision, and cleanup helpers, while correcting Yoshi motion-var struct usage and adding item header prototypes for Yoshi egg throw cleanup. The PR also refined ftYoshiAttributes offsets with named padding and a size static assert, making later matches safer.
what_changed: Implemented multiple formerly stubbed Yoshi functions in ftYs_SpecialN.c, including fn_8012D230, fn_8012D2F8, fn_8012D3C0, fn_8012D488, ftYs_SpecialS_8012E270, ftYs_SpecialAirSStart_0_Phys, ftYs_SpecialAirSEnd_Phys, ftYs_SpecialAirSStart_1_Phys, ftYs_SpecialAirSLanding_Phys, and ftYs_SpecialAirSEnd_Coll.
Added ityoshieggthrow.h include to ftYs_SpecialN.c so Yoshi special cleanup can call it_802B2890 on fp->fv.ys.x2238.
ftYs_SpecialS_8012E270 now checks fp->fv.ys.x2238 and fp->mv.ys.specials.x0, calls it_802B2890 when appropriate, clears the held item pointer, and clears take_dmg_cb and death2_cb.
Corrected motion-var field usage in SpecialHi animation from fp->mv.ys.specialn.x4 to fp->mv.ys.specialhi.x4.
Corrected SpecialAirS IASA code to clear fp->mv.ys.specials.x30 instead of fp->mv.ys.specialn.x30.
Expanded union ftYoshi_MotionVars so specialn, specials, and specialhi no longer incorrectly share fields: specials now owns x0 and x30 at fp+2370, while specialhi owns x4 at fp+2344.
Changed ftYoshi_FighterVars.x2238 from u32 to Item_GObj*, matching its use as an item object pointer.

Refined ftYoshiAttributes layout by replacing generic filler with offset-based padding, exposing x6C and x70 floats used by SpecialAirS physics, and adding STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct _ftYoshiAttributes) == 0x138).
Added several ityoshieggthrow.h prototypes around the 802B2890 item functions, with known prototypes for it_802B2890(Item_GObj*) and existing bool-return item callbacks preserved.
smart_moves: The PR fixed struct/union ownership before relying on fields in matched code, avoiding misleading matches that write through the wrong special-move variant.
Typing fp->fv.ys.x2238 as Item_GObj* enabled a direct, type-correct call to it_802B2890 rather than leaving the item reference as an integer-like placeholder.
Using offset-calculated padding such as pad_x50[0x6C - 0x50] and a sizeof static assert made ftYoshiAttributes safer for future field discovery.

The matched transition helpers preserve current animation frame through Fighter_ChangeMotionState and then call ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF00, reflecting a reusable pattern for related Yoshi SpecialS state transitions.

The physics matches exposed repeated patterns: zero velocity/acceleration using ftYs_Init_804D9A70 for one start state, ground friction plus ftCommon_8007CB74 for an end state, and ftCommon_8007D494 with character attributes x6C/x70 for airborne/landing physics.
decomp_lessons: When a move file contains several special variants, verify the motion-var union member before assigning fields; SpecialN, SpecialS, and SpecialHi can share offsets but represent different semantic fields.
If a fighter var is passed to an item function, promote the field from u32/UNK to Item_GObj* and include the appropriate item forward/header rather than casting everywhere.
Offset-based padding plus STATIC_ASSERT is preferable to large anonymous filler when newly discovered attributes must match exact offsets.
Small state-change wrapper functions can often match by preserving fp->cur_anim_frame, using a fixed motion-state ID and flags, and calling the shared per-state setup routine afterward.
Cleanup functions should clear both object references and callbacks after handling the item/resource, as shown by ftYs_SpecialS_8012E270 clearing x2238, take_dmg_cb, and death2_cb.
naming_conventions: Yoshi fighter special functions use the ftYs_ prefix, with SpecialS and SpecialAirS names for side-special states despite the PR title mentioning SpecialN.
Unknown matched helpers in this area retained address-based names such as fn_8012D230 until a stronger semantic name is available.
Yoshi motion-var union members are lowercase move names: specialn, specials, and specialhi.
New padding in ftYoshiAttributes uses offset-oriented names like pad_x50 and pad_x124 rather than generic data_filler names.
Item functions in ityoshieggthrow.h retain address-style names such as it_802B2890 when no descriptive symbol is available.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used exact motion-state constants such as 0x161, 0x15C, 0x163, and 0x15E with flags 0x0C4C5092U to match transition wrappers.
Preserved fp->cur_anim_frame in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, with start frame 0 and anim speed 1.
Used ftCommon_8007D5D4 for ground-related transitions and ftCommon_8007D7FC for air-related transitions before changing motion state.
Introduced an extern float ftYs_Init_804D9A70 path for zeroing several velocity/acceleration fields, mirroring an existing note that replacing a data symbol with a literal can prevent 100% matching.
Used M2C_FIELD only where still needed in existing code, while replacing other ambiguous accesses with structured fields such as fp->mv.ys.specials.x30 and fp->mv.ys.specialhi.x4.
Matched collision behavior by checking ft_80082708(gobj) == GA_Ground, then converting to ground state and calling ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC with current animation frame.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR context reports one issue comment but the human text excerpt is empty, so no actionable reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename fn_8012D230, fn_8012D2F8, fn_8012D3C0, or fn_8012D488 to semantic Yoshi SpecialS transition names.
Search for additional ftYoshiAttributes fields near offsets x6C and x70 to confirm their semantic names.
Inspect later changes to ityoshieggthrow.h for typed prototypes replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS around it_802B28C8 through it_802B2D30.
Compare other fighter special move files for the pattern of clearing item pointers and take_dmg_cb/death2_cb during cleanup.
Search for remaining M2C_FIELD accesses around fp+2370 in Yoshi code that could now use fp->mv.ys.specials.x30.
